Understanding PostgreSQL Indexing

PostgreSQL indexes speed up queries by letting the database find rows without scanning an entire table. The most common index type is the B-tree, which works well for equality and range lookups on ordered data.

For full-text search, PostgreSQL offers GIN indexes over tsvector columns. For fuzzy matching, the pg_trgm extension provides trigram indexes. Choosing the right index for each query pattern is the key to good performance as your dataset grows.
